title: landing_note
description: The free-form landing agent's close-out note. The landing is exploratory work, not a structured artifact: the only required field is a one-line `summary` of what the agent did or found this turn. Everything else the agent learned lives in its trace + any files it touched (under a write-mode grant). This minimal, permissive schema satisfies the engine's host.agent.task acceptance contract (loader M6b requires a schema) while keeping the output as close to 'free' as the runtime allows — additionalProperties is open so the agent may attach anything.

summary.description: One line: what the agent did or found this turn.

details.description: Optional longer markdown narrative of the exploration / change.

next_steps.description: Optional suggested follow-ups (e.g. a pipeline to drive, a file to review).

route.description: Optional on-path bail. Set ONLY when the request clearly belongs in a named pipeline reachable from the workbench (bugfix / implementation / cypilot / PR refinement / code review / PRD / design) rather than ad-hoc work the agent should just do here. When set, the workbench offers the operator a one-click jump onto that path — it is OFFERED, never forced. Leave unset for work the agent can carry out in place.

intent.description: The workbench navigation intent that enters the pipeline.

ticket_id.description: Optional — the ticket the pipeline should pick up, if the agent identified an existing one. Required for bugfix/implementation/cypilot/drive to clear their 'pick a ticket first' guard.

ticket_type.description: Optional — the picked ticket's type. Only consulted when intent is 'drive' (which routes by type).

label.description: Human one-liner for the bail button, e.g. 'Fix this as a bugfix ticket'.

reason.description: Why this path fits — shown above the button.
